race.comment: DIVINE THOUGHTS (7) resumed with a sound effort for sixth on a heavy track at Mornington and should derive plenty of benefit from that outing. Her record second-up is encouraging, and with a drop in grade, she is expected to improve sharply, especially with a 3kg claim. PLEASE ME DO (12) never threatened at Seymour after settling near the rear, but the run was better than it reads on paper. This field holds no fears for her, and she has the ability to make her presence felt. TOMMYESS (13) made the most of a change of tactics when finishing runner-up at Casterton 2 weeks ago, and he may try to lead them all the way here in his first run on the poly track. Stick with him. BAMA SLAMA (9) was only seventh last start, but his only win came on a poly track, so he is worth another chance. SUPERSET (1) fought on bravely for second at Sale 5 weeks ago, and he is worth including in all combos here.
